source_paper_title: Invariant States of Hydrodynamic Limits of Randomized Load-Balancing Networks
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Agarwal_2025, title={Invariant States of Hydrodynamic Limits of Randomized Load-Balancing Networks}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2020.0282}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2020.0282}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Agarwal, Pooja and Ramanan, Kavita}, year={2025}, month=jun }
context:
Section 1.1 (Introduction), page 3.

Since the N-server stationary distributions are known to be tight (due to the results in [5]), to provide a rigorous proof of this convergence it would suffice to show that the solution to the hydrodynamic equations converges to the invariant state characterized here for a large class of initial conditions. Whereas this is a non-trivial problem that is relegated to future work, it is encouraging that analogous results have been recently established for a related problem.
title: Prove convergence of hydrodynamic equations to the physical invariant state
background_latex:
Consider the SQ\((d)\) randomized load balancing model with a fixed integer \(d\ge 2\) and arrival rate \(\lambda\in(0,1)\). Jobs arrive to a system of \(N\) parallel single-server queues, each operating under FCFS service, with i.i.d. service times drawn from a distribution \(G\) on \([0,\infty)\) with finite mean normalized to 1. Upon arrival, \(d\) servers are sampled uniformly at random and the job joins the shortest among them.

In the many-server limit \(N	o\infty\), the system state can be described by a countable collection of finite measures \(
u(t)=(
u_\ell(t))_{\ell\in\mathbb N}\), where \(
u_\ell(t)\) is a finite measure on \([0,L)\) (with \(L\) the right end of the support of \(G\)) that places mass at the ages of jobs in service at queues of length at least \(\ell\) at time \(t\). Let \( ar G(x)=1-G(x)\), let \(g\) be the density of \(G\), and let the hazard rate be \(h(x)=g(x)/ ar G(x)\) for \(x\in[0,L)\). Define \(s_\ell(t)=\langle 1,
u_\ell(t)angle\), interpreted as the fraction of queues with length at least \(\ell\) in the limit.

The hydrodynamic limit is characterized by a deterministic evolution (the hydrodynamic equations) for \(
u(t)\) in terms of the departure functionals
\[D_\ell(t)=\int_0^t \langle h,
u_\ell(s)angle\,ds,\qquad \ell\in\mathbb N,\]
and an arrival-to-level-\(\ell\) measure \(\eta_\ell(t)\) defined using
\[P_d(x,y)= rac{x^d-y^d}{x-y}=\sum_{m=0}^{d-1} x^m y^{d-1-m},\qquad 0\le y\le x\le 1,\]
so that for \(\ell=1\), \(\eta_1(t)=\lambda\,(1-s_1(t))^d\,\delta_0\), and for \(\ell\ge 2\),
\[\eta_\ell(t)=\lambda\,P_d(s_{\ell-1}(t),s_\ell(t))\, ig(
u_{\ell-1}(t)-
u_\ell(t) ig),\]
with \(\delta_0\) the unit point mass at age 0.

An invariant state is a fixed point \(
u^*\) such that the constant trajectory \(
u(t)\equiv 
u^*\) satisfies the hydrodynamic equations. The paper characterizes (and shows existence of) a class of "physical" invariant states satisfying \(\lim_{\ell	o\infty} s^*_\ell=0\), where \(s^*_\ell=\langle 1,
u^*_\ellangle\).

Separately, for each finite \(N\), the \(N\)-server SQ\((d)\) system has a steady-state distribution \(\pi_N\) (under \(\lambda<1\), each finite-\(N\) system is ergodic). A natural conjecture is that as \(N	o\infty\), these steady states converge to the physical invariant state \(
u^*\) of the hydrodynamic equations. One route to such an interchange-of-limits result (first \(N	o\infty\), then \(t	o\infty\)) is to show that, for a large class of initial conditions \(
u(0)\), the solution \(
u(t)\) to the hydrodynamic equations converges as \(t	o\infty\) to \(
u^*\).
problem_definition_latex:
Establish conditions under which the solution \(
u(t)=(
u_\ell(t))_{\ell\in\mathbb N}\) to the hydrodynamic equations with arrival rate \(\lambda\in(0,1)\) and initial condition \(
u(0)\in S\) satisfies
\[
u(t)	o 
u^* \quad	ext{as } t	o\infty,\]
where \(
u^*\) is the physical invariant state characterized in the paper (equivalently, with \(s^*_\ell=\langle 1,
u^*_\ellangle\) satisfying \(\lim_{\ell	o\infty} s^*_\ell=0\)). Convergence should hold for a "large class" of initial conditions \(
u(0)\) (as specified/allowed by the hydrodynamic state space \(S\)).

open_problem_quote: Since the N-server stationary distributions are known to be tight (due to the results in [5]), to provide a rigorous proof of this convergence it would suffice to show that the solution to the hydrodynamic equations converges to the invariant state characterized here for a large class of initial conditions. Whereas this is a non-trivial problem that is relegated to future work, it is encouraging that analogous results have been recently established for a related problem.
subject_classification: Queueing theory; hydrodynamic/fluid limits; asymptotic equilibrium analysis
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for a general convergence-to-equilibrium theorem for an infinite-dimensional deterministic measure-valued dynamical system, not a finite combinatorial statement amenable to automated instance search.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: While one can numerically simulate the hydrodynamic equations for specific service distributions, proving convergence for a broad class of initial conditions lacks an automatic, reliable fitness signal and would require substantial analytical insight beyond programmatic evaluation.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
